feedback_text:
DIAGNOSIS: 38 failing sub-attributes (58.9 pts recoverable), 4 passing.

DO NOT CHANGE (these sections are passing):
  ✓ stride_regulatory_compliance_alignment: 100% — strong, lower priority
  ✓ mobile_crypto_key_management: 100% — strong, lower priority
  ✓ threat_intelligence_integration: 100% — strong, lower priority
  ✓ threat_risk_mitigation_coherence: 100% — strong, lower priority

REQUIRED FIXES (ordered by point impact):

  FIX 1: mobile_platform_owasp_coverage.owasp_mobile_coverage (current: 25%, 2.4 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: Content addresses mobile security threats but lacks explicit OWASP Mobile Top 10 framework mapping or systematic coverage.
    Measurement spec: 1.0 if 8+ categories addressed with technical detail, 0.7 if 6-7 categories, 0.4 if 4-5 categories, 0.0 if <4 categories
    Evidence found: Addresses 8+ OWASP Mobile Top 10 categories: not found in content; References OWASP Mobile Top 10 explicitly: not found in content; Goes beyond rubric with OWASP mapping: no evidence of independent insight
    ✗ FAILED CHECK: Addresses 8+ OWASP Mobile Top 10 categories
      Evidence: not found in content
    ✗ FAILED CHECK: References OWASP Mobile Top 10 explicitly
      Evidence: not found in content
    ✗ FAILED CHECK: Goes beyond rubric with OWASP mapping
      Evidence: no evidence of independent insight
    → ACTION: Map threats to specific OWASP Mobile Top 10 categories (M1-M10) and ensure coverage of at least 8 categories with technical implementation details.

  FIX 2: stride_threat_coverage_depth.mobile_banking_specificity (current: 75%, 2.4 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: Content demonstrates strong mobile banking domain expertise with 73% of threats showing specific financial services context, including biometric attacks and P2P payment manipulation.
    Measurement spec: 1.0 if 60%+ threats show mobile banking expertise (jailbreak detection bypass, secure element attacks, PCI tokenization vulnerabilities), 0.7 if 40-59%, 0.4 if 20-39%, 0.0 if <20%
    Evidence found: Threats include secure element attacks: not found in content; Threats include PCI tokenization vulnerabilities: not found in content
    ✗ FAILED CHECK: Threats include secure element attacks
      Evidence: not found in content
    ✗ FAILED CHECK: Threats include PCI tokenization vulnerabilities
      Evidence: not found in content
    → ACTION: Add threats targeting secure element key extraction and PCI tokenization bypass to reach exceptional mobile banking specificity.
    ⚖ TRADEOFF: DO NOT sacrifice 'quantitative_risk_assessment_methodology' for 'stride_threat_coverage_depth'. When these conflict, 'quantitative_risk_assessment_methodology' wins unconditionally. Risk assessment quality takes precedence - better to have fewer threats with rigorous quantitative analysis than comprehensive coverage with shallow scoring

  FIX 3: regulatory_compliance_threat_integration.compliance_to_technical_mapping (current: 0%, 2.0 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: Content lacks any explicit mapping between regulatory compliance requirements and technical threat analysis.

    Evidence found: All regulatory controls have corresponding technical threat analysis: No regulatory controls explicitly referenced; PCI DSS requirements mapped to technical threats: not found in content; PSD2 requirements have technical analysis: not found in content; Goes beyond rubric with comprehensive compliance mapping: no evidence of independent insight

    ✗ FAILED CHECK: PSD2 requirements have technical analysis

    → ACTION: Add explicit mapping of PCI DSS 3.4.1 cryptographic requirements to mobile key extraction threats, and PSD2 strong authentication to biometric spoofing analysis.

  FIX 4: regulatory_compliance_threat_integration.technical_to_compliance_mapping (current: 0%, 2.0 pts at stake)

  FIX 10: stride_threat_coverage_depth.category_completeness (current: 50%, 1.8 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: Content covers all STRIDE categories but falls short of minimum 4 threats per category requirement, with only 11 total threats instead of required 24.
    Measurement spec: 1.0 if 4+ threats per category with balanced distribution, 0.7 if 3+ threats per category with minor gaps, 0.4 if 2+ threats per category with significant gaps, 0.0 if any STRIDE category missing or <2 threats
    Evidence found: Minimum 4 distinct threats per STRIDE category (24 total): Only 11 total threats identified: S1, S2, T1, T2, R1, I1, I2, D1, D2, E1, E2; Balanced distribution across all six STRIDE categories: 2 Spoofing, 2 Tampering, 1 Repudiation, 2 Information Disclosure, 2 Denial of Service, 2 Elevation of Privilege

    ✗ FAILED CHECK: Balanced distribution across all six STRIDE categories
      Evidence: 2 Spoofing, 2 Tampering, 1 Repudiation, 2 Information Disclosure, 2 Denial of Service, 2 Elevation of Privilege
    → ACTION: Add 13 more threats to reach minimum 24 total, ensuring each STRIDE category has at least 4 distinct threats. For example, add more Spoofing threats like certificate pinning bypass, fake SSL certificates, and OAuth token spoofing.
    ⚖ TRADEOFF: DO NOT sacrifice 'quantitative_risk_assessment_methodology' for 'stride_threat_coverage_depth'. When these conflict, 'quantitative_risk_assessment_methodology' wins unconditionally. Risk assessment quality takes precedence - better to have fewer threats with rigorous quantitative analysis than comprehensive coverage with shallow scoring
